original_hypothesis.title: Router update is bricked: setRouter only allows zero address, enabling permanent launch DoS if called (or preventing urgent router migration)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GenesisPoolManager.setRouter(address) contains an inverted require that only permits setting router to address(0). If executed (accidentally or maliciously via privileged owner), subsequent launch() calls will revert (router is zero). Even if not executed, the bug prevents legitimate router upgrades/migration, creating operational fragility.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrable code defect affecting critical operations (genesis launches). While it requires owner action, it is a realistic ops failure mode during incident response or upgrade attempts.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setRouter (address _router) external onlyOwner { require(_router == address(0), "ZA"); router = _router; }

preconditions: setRouter is called with address(0) (only passing value)
Genesis pools rely on GenesisPoolManager.router during _launchPool

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Setter intended to update router cannot set a valid router

operational_questions: Who holds the owner() role for GenesisPoolManager in production?
Is router already immutable elsewhere or is this the only update path?
Are there emergency procedures that would attempt to rotate router during incidents?
validation_requirements: Confirm deployed GenesisPoolManager bytecode contains the inverted require
Simulate launch with router==0 to confirm full lifecycle DoS
Review operational runbooks/scripts for router changes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Epoch flip attempts launch; GenesisPool.launch calls IRouter(0).addLiquidity → revert
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Pools stuck in PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED; user funds operationally frozen until manual interventions
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Protocol cannot migrate router via this setter due to the bug

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Genesis launches halted; potential widespread user funds lock-in until governance/upgrade
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High if no upgrade path or if upgrades require governance that is itself compromised

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Mass launch failures at epoch flip
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Router change attempts reverting when non-zero provided

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
